title: Cuticle oil
subtitle: A light jojoba and vitamin E blend for dry cuticles and nail beds
excerpt: Jojoba is the closest plant-based substance to human sebum and absorbs quickly without leaving a greasy residue. Combined with vitamin E as an antioxidant and rose geranium for fragrance, this oil softens the cuticle and conditions the nail bed. It applies from a dropper or roller-ball bottle.
sourceNotes: Cuticle oil formulation from Tisserand Institute (tisserandinstitute.org, May 2026). Jojoba properties from supplier datasheet. Rose geranium EO safety from Essential Oil Safety, Tisserand and Young (2014).

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally to fill as many dropper bottles as needed. 30 ml per bottle is a comfortable quantity.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make in advance and store in a cool, dark place. Apply to each cuticle before bed and massage in gently.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 2 years. Jojoba has one of the longest shelf lives of any carrier oil. Vitamin E extends the shelf life further. Store away from direct sunlight. No water content means no preservative is required.

recipeTools.notes: Or any small jug for blending and pouring

glossaryTerms.definition: A plant-derived oil used to dilute essential oils and deliver them to the skin. Carrier oils have their own skin-conditioning properties. Jojoba, sweet almond, and fractionated coconut oil are among the most common choices in cosmetic formulation.

content.text: Jojoba is a liquid wax ester, not a true oil. Its molecular structure is close to the skin's own sebum, so it absorbs quickly and leaves no greasy residue. As a

for cuticle work, it is one of the most effective choices. It penetrates the nail bed and the skin around the nail, softens the cuticle, and leaves no film on the nails that would interfere with polish.

Vitamin E is added at 0.5% as an antioxidant to extend the blend's shelf life. Rose geranium essential oil adds fragrance at a skin-safe 1% dilution. This formula is safe for use during pregnancy at this dilution rate.

Weigh all three ingredients into a small jug or bowl. Stir gently to combine. Pour into a clean dropper bottle or roller-ball bottle. Label with the date. To use, apply one to two drops to each cuticle and massage in with a fingertip. Apply before bed for best results.
2 years in a cool, dark place. No water is present, so no preservative is required. Keep the dropper tip clean and replace the cap after each use.
